title: Quantum Accelerated Model Selection: Leveraging Hoeffding Races in Quantum Computing for Enhanced Classification and Function Approximation
abstract: The computational demands of model selection via cross-validation pose significant challenges, particularly with large datasets and numerous candidate models. Quantum computing offers a promising avenue to expedite these processes. This paper explores the integration of Hoeffding Races, a technique for efficient model selection, with quantum computing frameworks to enhance classification and function approximation tasks. We propose a quantum-accelerated variant of Hoeffding Races that leverages quantum parallelism and quantum annealing to rapidly discard suboptimal models and focus computational resources on promising candidates. Our methodology combines quantum algorithms with classical machine learning techniques, demonstrating significant speedup in model selection on quantum simulators. Empirical results on benchmark datasets illustrate the potential of this approach, highlighting improvements in both accuracy and computational efficiency. The findings suggest that quantum computing can substantially mitigate the computational bottlenecks in model selection, paving the way for more scalable and effective machine learning applications.
